ROI question for AI agent platforms is usually framed as 6-12 month payback.

For ONE specific agent in Avery NXR's template library, payback is one week. Sometimes one day.

The agent: Marcus, the resume screening agent. Math + why this single agent pays for the whole

One of the harder things about positioning Avery NXR: "who uses it" looks different across customer segments.

Solo consultant uses it differently than 50-person team. 50-person team uses it differently than 500-person regulated enterprise.

Same product. Three very
tweets.name: Avery.Software

tweets.text:
1/10

Avery NXR ships with 63 connectors: 15 OAuth + 48 API-key across 13 categories.

Specific number. Not 50, not 100. Behind it = a real prioritization story.

Here's why 63 and not 1,000. 🧵
tweets.name: Avery.Software

tweets.text:
1/10

When we built Avery NXR, Excel/workbook bidirectional sync was a "nice to have." Mentioned on website. Didn't lead with it.

3 months in, it's one of the most-used features by deployed customers.

We've reordered marketing because of how heavily it shows up. Here's why. 🧵
tweets.name: Avery.Software

tweets.text:
1/10

Product strategy is usually framed in terms of what you ARE building.

The less-discussed half: what you're DELIBERATELY not building.

Here's what Avery NXR is NOT and why each "no" makes the product better. 🧵
tweets.name: Avery.Software

tweets.text:
1/10

We get asked often: Avery NXR vs CrewAI?

Honest answer: they're not really competitors. They're solving adjacent problems for different audiences.

Here's the framing that makes the difference clear. 🧵
tweets.name: Avery.Software

tweets.text:
1/10

Prospects evaluating Avery NXR ask about:
→ Templates
→ Connectors
→ Pricing
→ Model selection

Rarely about: the audit ledger.

Then they deploy. Two months in, the audit ledger is the feature they bring up in every renewal conversation.

Here's why. 🧵
tweets.name: Avery.Software

tweets.text:
1/10

Avery NXR runs local models through Ollama.

This sometimes surprises engineers who'd expect us to use vLLM or another "production-grade" runtime.

Here's the reasoning behind that choice. 🧵
tweets.name: Avery.Software

tweets.text:
1/10

We gave our Avery NXR agents Slack access 3 months ago.

The change was bigger than expected.

When you put an agent INSIDE Slack instead of in a separate interface, the relationship inverts. Agent is where the team already is. 🧵
tweets.name: Avery.Software

tweets.text:
1/10

We watched how Avery NXR users schedule their agents.

The single most common cron expression: between 2-6 AM local time. Peak at 4 AM.

Nobody chose 4 AM because their morning starts then. They chose 4 AM so their work is waiting when their morning DOES start.
tweets.name: Avery.Software
